threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system utilizes default configurations for authentication, potentially exposing it to brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or password reuse vulnerabilities. Lack of multi-factor authentication (MFA) further exacerbates this risk. Attackers could impersonate legitimate users, gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data or functionality.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of default credentials, password cracking, or credential stuffing attacks targeting the authentication service.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Regularly monitor authentication logs for suspicious activity and implement account lockout policies.

threats.title: Default Account Vulnerability
threats.description: If the system utilizes default accounts with elevated privileges, attackers could exploit these accounts to gain unauthorized access to sensitive system functions or data.  Lack of proper account management practices exacerbates this risk.
threats.affected_component: System Servers, Database Servers, Application Servers
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting default credentials or vulnerabilities associated with privileged accounts.
mitigations: Immediately change all default passwords.
Disable or remove default accounts.
Implement role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict user privileges.

threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: The system likely uses a database to store information, and if input validation is inadequate, attackers could inject malicious SQL code into input fields. This could allow them to modify, delete, or extract data from the database, potentially compromising the entire system.
threats.attack_vector: Malicious SQL code injected into user input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes, data entry fields).
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user inputs.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: The system could be vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks if it processes malicious input that consumes excessive system resources (CPU, memory, bandwidth). This could render the system unavailable to legitimate users.
threats.affected_component: Web Application Server, Database Server, Network Infrastructure
threats.attack_vector: Attackers sending specially crafted requests designed to overwhelm the system's resources.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Use a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic.
Optimize the system's code and database queries to reduce resource consumption.

threats.title: Insufficient Data Classification
threats.description: Without knowing the sensitivity of the data stored in the centralized database, it's impossible to determine the appropriate level of access control. Sensitive data could be exposed to unauthorized users or systems if access controls are not properly configured. This includes exposing internal system details, user data, or business logic.
threats.affected_component: Database Server, Application Servers, Network Infrastructure
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to the database due to overly permissive access controls or misconfigured network security.
mitigations: Conduct a data classification exercise to identify and categorize data based on sensitivity.
Implement the principle of least privilege, granting users only the access they need to perform their jobs.
Regularly review and update access controls to ensure they remain appropriate.

Lack of multi-factor authentication (MFA) further exacerbates this risk. Attackers could impersonate legitimate users, gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data or functionality.

gaps: Weak Authentication Mechanism - Insufficient focus on multi-factor authentication (MFA) across all systems.
Insufficient Data Classification - Limited controls addressing data loss prevention (DLP) and data masking.
priorities: High - SQL Injection Vulnerability & Default Account Vulnerability - Immediate remediation required due to high severity and potential for significant damage.
Medium - Weak Authentication Mechanism & Resource Exhaustion - Requires prompt attention to mitigate risks.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,auth_service,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Default Account Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insufficient Data Classification
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
